channel.title: Maritime Shipping Updates
channel.description: Latest incidents, safety issues, health alerts, and regulatory updates from the cruise and maritime shipping industry.

item.title: Cruise Passenger Dies After Going Overboard from Carnival Liberty in Bahamas
item.dc:creator: Jim Walker
item.description:
Incident: A man died after jumping from his balcony on the Carnival Liberty while sailing from Celebration Key to Nassau, Bahamas.

Crew located and retrieved him, but he did not survive. The ship altered course for approximately one hour during search and rescue.

This is the 437th recorded overboard incident since 2000.

Source images and tracking: Carnival Liberty location map and incident timeline available in original report.

Images:


  • Carnival Liberty Location Map showing course change during search and rescue (The Cruise Globe)

media:content.media:description: Carnival Liberty Location Map (The Cruise Globe)

item.title: Over 200 Foreign Crew Members Deported by U.S. Authorities Over Alleged CSAM Cases
item.dc:creator: Jim Walker
item.description:
Incident: U.S. CBP and ICE conducted raids on multiple cruise ships, including Disney vessels, detaining and deporting over 200 crew members (primarily Filipino) accused of viewing child sexual abuse material (CSAM).

Most were deported without criminal prosecution. Reports highlight concerns over due process, lack of evidence transparency, and treatment of crew.

Images:


  • Disney Magic — David T. Flaherty, CC BY 2.0 commons/wikimedia

  • Video screenshot: CBP escorting Disney crew members into immigration vans — Dharmi Mehta

  • Detained crew members — DFO Florida and Customs and Border Protection

media:content.media:description: CBP detaining Disney crew members on dock in San Diego

item.title: Norwegian Dawn Fails CDC Health Inspection
item.dc:creator: Jim Walker
item.description:
Incident: Norwegian Dawn received an unsatisfactory CDC score of 84/100 during an inspection in Tampa, Florida.

Violations included fruit flies in buffet areas, mold in ice machines, refrigeration failures, and unknown liquid dripping in food prep zones.

Norwegian submitted corrective actions. This was the first cruise ship failure in 2026.

No images were published with the original article.

item.title: Norwegian Getaway Passenger Dies While Snorkeling in Bahamas
item.dc:creator: Jim Walker
item.description:
Incident: An 83-year-old American passenger died while snorkeling at Great Stirrup Cay (Norwegian's private island) on May 3.

He was found unresponsive by his son. Local authorities ruled it an apparent drowning.

No images were published with the original article.

item.title: Norovirus Outbreak Sickens 115 on Caribbean Princess
item.dc:creator: Jim Walker
item.description:
Incident: 102 passengers and 13 crew members sickened by norovirus on a 13-day Caribbean cruise (3.3% of passengers affected).

Ship remains at sea until May 11. This is the fourth GI outbreak reported by CDC in 2026.

No images were published with the original article.

item.title: Woman Assaults Teen on Carnival Sunshine, Walks Off Ship
item.dc:creator: Jim Walker
item.description:
Incident: Jennifer Frost, 44, allegedly punched a teenager in the face, breaking her nose, on the Carnival Sunshine in March.

She was detained but allowed to disembark. She later surrendered in Miami.

No images were published with the original article.

item.title: Woman Identified in Fatal Fall on Carnival Firenze
item.dc:creator: Jim Walker
item.description:
Incident: 24-year-old Brianna Miller fell from her balcony on the Carnival Firenze near Catalina Island, California, on April 27.

FBI is investigating; foul play not ruled out.

No inline images were published with the original article. Photo credit noted as Wikipedia (Carnival Firenze, formerly Costa Firenze).

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreak Kills Three on Expedition Ship Hondius
item.dc:creator: Jim Walker
item.description:
Incident: Three passengers dead and several others sick from suspected hantavirus on the Hondius. Ship is stranded off Cape Verde.

Two crew members still awaiting medical evacuation. First known hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship.

Images:


  • Hondius location stranded outside Praia, Cape Verde (The Cruise Globe)

media:content.media:description: Hondius ship location off Cape Verde (The Cruise Globe)

item.title: Crew Member Goes Overboard from Norwegian Breakaway off Cape Cod
item.dc:creator: Jim Walker
item.description:
Incident: A galley crew member (Indian national) went overboard from the Norwegian Breakaway off Wellfleet, Massachusetts on April 25.

Ship conducted a 4-hour search before resuming course. U.S. Coast Guard assisted.

Images:


  • Norwegian Breakaway Search and Rescue Operation — ship tracking during course change (The Cruise Globe)

media:content.media:description: Norwegian Breakaway Search and Rescue Operation tracking map (The Cruise Globe)

item.title: Q1 2026 Cruise Crime Report: Royal Caribbean Leads With 14 Incidents
item.dc:creator: Jim Walker
item.description:
Summary: 43 crimes reported across cruise lines in Q1 2026. Royal Caribbean had 14, Carnival 11. Sexual assaults remain the dominant category.

Data is known to be underreported due to self-reporting by cruise lines.

Images:


  • Historical cruise crime data graph — reported crimes across all cruise lines since 2016, showing nearly twofold increase through 2025

media:content.media:description: Historical Cruise Crime Data chart showing reported crimes by year
